hypothesis: Introducing aromatic substitutions at position 5 and charge-optimized residues at position 6 will enhance SSTR2-specific complementarity with ECL2/TM5 while reducing off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that mutations at non-pharmacophore positions can disrupt off-target interactions while maintaining SSTR2 binding. This hypothesis is supported by the best Δmargin values but needs refinement to increase the pass rate and overall selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are known to contribute to off-target interactions with SSTR3 and SSTR5. Focusing mutations here can disrupt conserved-pocket contacts while preserving pharmacophore positions.
These mutations are designed to break conserved interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 without affecting the pharmacophore core (positions 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
